title: Fix hero section to display the wide banner image (Image 1)

requirements.text: Save the uploaded wide banner image (A1EB606A-8094-4BB7-93B6-3C6A9B809C15-2.png — navy background with Chicago skyline, plumber mascot shield logo, and 'I GOT A DOM' text) as the hero background image at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1334x750.png and render it as the full-width hero section background on the Home page.

quotes: make sure that the image was working on the hero page
the wide banner with the Chicago skyline background — as the hero image

acceptanceCriteria: The wide banner image (navy/Chicago skyline/plumber mascot) is visible as the hero section background on the Home page
The image spans the full width of the hero section without being cut off or misaligned
The image is saved to frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1334x750.png and referenced via a static asset path
The hero section displays correctly on both desktop and mobile viewports

constraints: Do not change the backend schema or any backend logic
Do not modify immutable frontend paths (hooks, main.tsx, ui components)
nonGoals: Redesigning any other section of the website
Using Image 2 (white background logo) as the hero image

required: Wide horizontal banner with a dark navy textured background, Chicago city skyline silhouette in the center-background, a plumber mascot in a shield badge on the left (man in navy cap and uniform with crossed arms holding a red pipe wrench), and bold text 'I GOT A DOM' in white and red on the right (hero-banner.dim_1334x750.png)
